key_insight: Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS) uses a prior-guided agent plus evolutionary and gradient optimization to discover interpretable symbolic PDE laws and approximations beyond manual derivations, meshes, or neural nets.
engineering_relevance: It offers an automated way to turn PDE theory and constraints into reusable analytical representations that can support model understanding, simulation guidance, and downstream design decisions.
method: Agent translates PDE theory, problem constraints, and prior search experience into differentiable symbolic programs.
Evolutionary search refines the symbolic form while gradient descent fits continuous parameters.
Acts as inductive-bias injection for recovering known formulas or constructing new analytical approximations.
evidence_signal: Across five PDE problems spanning bounded dynamics, finite-time blow-up, and free-boundary focusing, ASYS produced interpretable formulas such as a geometric interface law for Allen-Cahn 2D dynamics and a nine-parameter contraction law for Keller-Segel blow-up.
watch_item: The approach depends on strong priors and search design, so its robustness and scalability to harder PDE families may be sensitive to the quality of symbolic constraints and optimization setup.
paper_title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
